narrative: Jindal Stainless was the clear leader across the air-emissions theme in FY2022-23, ranking 1st on SOx, NOx and PM. Its values were 0.85189 kt SOx, 1.9807 kt NOx and 2.0841 kt PM, structurally far below Tata Steel and JSW. This reflects both scale and process-route differences: Jindal’s stainless-steel operations are smaller and more EAF/scrap-linked, while Tata and JSW operate larger integrated carbon-steel systems with higher sinter, coke, blast furnace, captive power and process-gas combustion exposure. JSW ranked 2nd across the three metrics, while Tata ranked 3rd across the three metrics. Tata FY2022-23 must be interpreted cautiously because the standardised table flags it as consolidated, including international operations, rather than being directly comparable to standalone Indian operations. SAIL was the laggard by ranking rule because SOx, NOx and PM were not reported in the standardised table for FY2022-23.
